updates.tweet_text:
Very simple session and summer trading is the same thing everyday in #ES_F. Support was 7570-73, given well in advance + in newsletter. We sold, and held *exact*. Targets were then 7597 (hit), 7604 (hit), 7611, 7621, 7640 next.

Ride runner now, and we do it again tomorrow https://t.co/7UD4ICssAx
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7570-73, 7597, 7604, 7611, 7621, 7640) are morning-plan reaffirmations or hit-target observations. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @T_Ngo_T SOmetimes volatility just not there in this context, fine to just take the long on the bounce out in these cases especially when its just a slow grind down. That's what I do (or can just directly buy it)
updates.notes: Pure trading advice/commentary on volatility and entry style; no specific price level named or flagged as actionable.

updates.tweet_text: @house2k6 I took it on the bounce out, seeing we didn't have the vol for the full failed breakdown
updates.notes: Trade management reply describing exit rationale; no price named, no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @mohzoheir If its clearly just defending then bouncing out you can just take the long. If it traps then can also take the long. Just need to react to whatever it does

updates.tweet_text: @PermaFader Yes, didn't have the volatility to sweep but defended nicely and started pushing. Easy long

updates.tweet_text: @PermaFader Slow grind into the zone as well

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: 7570-73 was support today, held exact, and off we went. Now just ride runner for 7640 which is the next magnet. NEwsletter out soon talking possible dip buy entries if we luck out and get another volatility spike this week. We are close to a *massive* move

updates.tweet_text: @NoBSOracle1 For me its always up. I don't "predict" downside moves ever because we are in an uptrend, and you'll never hear me say anything bearish
